name: SYNB1618 showed human tolerability and phenylalanine-metabolite pharmacodynamics
summary: The SYNB1618 trial supports engineered live-biotherapeutic feasibility in humans but is disease-specific and not aging-directed.
statement: In healthy volunteers and adults with phenylketonuria, the engineered E. coli Nissle strain SYNB1618 was safe and well tolerated up to a maximum tolerated dose and produced dose-responsive phenylalanine-metabolite pharmacodynamic signals.
population_or_model: Healthy adult volunteers and adults with phenylketonuria
time_horizon: Single-dose and up to 7-day dosing cohorts
quantitative_note: The study enrolled 56 healthy volunteers and 14 patients with phenylketonuria; the reported maximum tolerated dose was 200 billion CFU, and all participants cleared the bacteria within 4 days after the last dose.
caveats: The indication was phenylketonuria, not aging or dysbiosis.
The primary value for this track is platform feasibility, safety, and pharmacodynamics.
Short-term dosing does not establish chronic-aging safety.
The study was sponsored by the therapeutic developer.
